english: 1. Cs. marble. 2. plaster-floor, made of pulverized marble and oil, also *'a-źál.* ('a zhal)
an offspring of parents not having the same rank, nor the same religion, and not belonging to the same nation Ld.
Sch.: 'the offering of sacrifices'; Will.: respectful offering to a god or Brāhman.
Ssk., Sch.: cotton, 'ar-dza-ka'i dóg-pa ('ar dza ka'i dog pa) Glr. cotton-capsule. (This signification is not to be found in our Ssk.-dictionaries.)
Sch.: 'to be reduced to extremities, to extreme misery' (?).
Sch.: 'the one half of a pair, e.g. one eye', = ya-yčig (ya gcig).
(for da-lta (da lta)) Bal. 1. now, at present. — 2. to-day.
*'al-tiṅ, *'al-tiṅ-la kur-cè* ('al ting la khur ce) W. to carry something bulky tied up in the girdle.
earthen kitchen-pot Ld.
v. 'a-la-la ('a la la).
Tar.: nór-bu asma-gar-bha (nor bu asma gar bha) Schf.: emerald.
1. beer, = čaṅ (chang), C. — 2. vulgar pronunciation of dbyi (dbyi), *yi* (yi), the lynx. — 3. num.: 60.
W. hiccough, sob, *'i-kug yoṅ-ṅa rag* ('i khug yong nga rag) I have got the hiccough, *'i-kug gyab dug* ('i khug rgyab dug) he hiccoughs.
cochineal; yet among the substances devoted to a costly Chodten it is mentioned as one of the five divine jewels Glr. 7.
sapphire (Sch.: emerald?).
with *co-ce* (co ce) W. to persuade, e.g. to buy something.
(Ssk. Ficus glomerata) in Tibetan literature a fabulous lotos of immense size.
Ssk., prob. also spelled dbú-ma (dbu ma), n. of the wife of Siwa (Durga, Kālī etc.).
Sch. = 'u-túg ('u thug).

1. also 'oḍiyana ('o Di ya na) Cs., (not mentioned in Ssk. dictionaries), often written in the abbreviated form ཨོརྒྱན, a fabulous country in the north-west of India (though Cs. supposes it to be Ujain), frequently represented as a kind of paradise. — 2. now a noun personal of frq. occurrence; 'u-rgyan-padma ('u rgyan padma) v. padma-byuṅ-gnás (padma byung gnas).
n. of a remedy Med.
oil-lamp C.
a blue lotos which is also used for medicinal purposes. In Lh. this name seems to be transferred to Polemonium caeruleum.
v. 'u-túg ('u thug).
a kiss, *'um jór-wa* ('um sbyor ba) or *gyág-pa* (rgyag pa) to kiss C.
a warm meal-porridge; fermenting dough C.
v. ur-rdó (ur rdo).
v. dbúr-ba (dbur ba).
col. for yyul-tág (yul thag).

1. in C. and later literature, an interrogative, pronounced short, accented, and usually put immediately before the vb. or the pron. which stands in the place of the vb.: *dè-mo ě yọ?* (bde mo e yo) do you feel well? are you well? are you getting on well? kyed daṅ 'e oprad mi śes (khyed dang e 'phrad mi shes) I do not know whether I shall see you again Mil.; 'e nus mi nus (e nus mi nus) whether we shall be able or not Mil.; rarely for even if, though, although, 'e sus kyaṅ mi túb-na (e sus kyang mi thub na) though nobody is really able to do it. — 2. num.: 120.
